context: Tests whether the agent applies the ticket-planning skill correctly: classifying each ticket with all required attributes, applying correct area title prefixes (BE |, FE |, Mobile |) with a space after the pipe, using the standard five-section ticket structure in the right order, keeping sections business-facing without cross-repeating content, and sequencing foundation/api tickets before client tickets.

checklist.name: All 8 classification attributes present
checklist.description: Every ticket in tickets.md lists all eight classification attributes: area, type, dependency_level, execution_order, coordination_need, external_dependency, urgency, target_bucket

checklist.name: area values from allowed set
checklist.description: Every ticket's 'area' value is one of: backend, web, mobile, cross-platform, external — no other values used

checklist.name: execution_order values from allowed set
checklist.description: Every ticket's 'execution_order' value is one of: foundation, api, client, follow-up

checklist.name: BE | prefix on backend tickets
checklist.description: All backend-area tickets have titles starting with 'BE | ' (with a space after the pipe) — at least one such ticket exists

checklist.name: FE | prefix on frontend tickets
checklist.description: All web-area tickets have titles starting with 'FE | ' (with a space after the pipe) — at least one such ticket exists

checklist.name: No prefix on non-area tickets
checklist.description: Any ticket classified as cross-platform or external does NOT have a BE |, FE |, or Mobile | prefix unless the content clearly belongs to one of those areas

checklist.name: Five sections present in each ticket
checklist.description: Every ticket body contains all five sections: Summary, Background, Acceptance Criteria, Dependencies, Technical Notes — in that order

checklist.name: Summary states outcome only
checklist.description: The Summary section in each ticket is a single statement of the desired outcome — it does NOT restate the Background or repeat Acceptance Criteria verbatim

checklist.name: Technical Notes scope correct
checklist.description: Technical Notes sections contain only implementation details relevant to sequencing or scoping — they do NOT repeat Acceptance Criteria items verbatim

checklist.name: Foundation/API before client sequencing
checklist.description: sequencing_notes.md explains that backend/API tickets (foundation or api execution_order) should be started before web or mobile client tickets

checklist.name: Draft-only output
checklist.description: tickets.md contains only markdown ticket drafts — there is no mention of creating issues in an external system, no issue keys, and no API calls described

checklist.name: target_bucket values from allowed set
checklist.description: Every ticket's 'target_bucket' value is one of: ready-to-refine, next-dev-sprint, later

checklist.name: type values from allowed set
checklist.description: Every ticket's 'type' value is either Story or Task
